key_insight: A static 5D PCYS interpolation table, periodically recalibrated with first-order Taylor linearization, can approximate VPSC-based irradiation-driven material response with high accuracy while dramatically reducing multi-scale simulation cost.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical FEM analysis of zirconium alloy nuclear components like spacer grids and fuel cladding over reactor lifetimes without the prohibitive expense of full crystal-plasticity coupling.
method: Embed a 5D interpolation table as a surrogate constitutive law inside Code_Aster.
Update the surrogate through periodic recalibration plus first-order Taylor linearization to track radiation-induced microstructural drift.
Benchmark the PCYS-FEM workflow against VPSC-FEM under continuous high-dose irradiation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the linearization scheme keeps relative errors below 1% for representative deformation paths and that spacer-grid simulations achieve meaningful computational savings.
watch_item: The approach depends on careful calibration and may lose accuracy if irradiation-driven state changes move outside the surrogate’s interpolation/linearization range.
paper_title: Accelerating Multi-scale Simulations of Nuclear Components via PCYS Interpolation Tables
